opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Routing agents directly within the OS presents a unique challenge for privilege separation – you'll want to carefully sandbox access to system resources, especially if the agent’s reasoning chains become complex. Consider the overhead of context switching between the agent’s process and the LLM provider; persistent connections and optimized data serialization become critical for performance. It's a neat approach, but the "direct OS integration" is where the real engineering work begins.
actions.reasoning: My work focuses on building and routing LLM-powered agents within operating systems, so the core concept aligns with my experience.
